metadata.title: Guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products for the treatment of chronic heart failure

rules.rule_text: Mortality/morbidity data should be provided prior to approval of new therapeutic agents for CHF treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, mortality/morbidity data should be provided prior to approval of new therapeutic agents for the treatment of CHF.
rules.exceptions: Special circumstances for functional status or PRO endpoints in selected populations (see 4.1.3, 4.1.4).

rules.rule_text: The main therapeutic goals for CHF treatment are to reduce cardiovascular mortality and prevent deterioration of clinical status and hospitalisations. These must represent the primary aim of new agents.
rules.verbatim_quote: The main therapeutic goals in the treatment of CHF are to reduce cardiovascular mortality and to prevent deterioration of the clinical status and hospitalisations; these goals should represent the primary aim of new agents developed for the treatment of CHF.
rules.exceptions: Improvement in functional capacity may be relevant in selected patients.

rules.rule_text: Therapeutic effects on symptoms and quality of life can only be interpreted without ambiguity in the absence of a detrimental effect on the main therapeutic goals (mortality/morbidity).
rules.verbatim_quote: The therapeutic effects on symptoms and quality of life are also of great importance to patients with CHF but they are more difficult to measure and have lower reproducibility and can be interpreted without ambiguity only in the absence of a detrimental effect on the main therapeutic goals.

rules.rule_text: Haemodynamic changes (LVEF, LV remodelling) and biomarkers are considered to provide only supportive data and cannot serve as primary endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: Haemodynamic changes (e.g. left ventricular ejection fraction [LVEF], left ventricular remodelling and biomarkers) are considered to provide only supportive data.
rules.exceptions: May be useful for mode of action elucidation in early phase studies.

rules.rule_text: Mortality must be considered as the primary endpoint either alone or as a component of a composite endpoint in combination with worsening heart failure endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: One of the main therapeutic goals in the treatment of CHF is to reduce mortality. Thus, mortality is to be considered as the primary endpoint either alone or as a component of a composite endpoint in combination with endpoint(s) related to worsening of heart failure (for special circumstances and alternatives see 4.1.3 and 4.1.4).
rules.exceptions: Alternatives for special circumstances in sections 4.1.3 and 4.1.4.

rules.rule_text: Assessment of mortality in confirmatory trials must include both all-cause mortality and cardiovascular mortality.
rules.verbatim_quote: Assessment of mortality in confirmatory trials should include both all-cause mortality and cardiovascular mortality (see section 5.1).

rules.rule_text: Worsening heart failure (WHF) may qualify as an episode managed either in hospital or on an outpatient basis by an emergency visit.
rules.verbatim_quote: An episode of worsening of heart failure (WHF) may qualify as an episode managed either in a hospital setting or on an outpatient basis by an emergency visit (see also 5.2).

rules.rule_text: If recurrent HFH events are included as a co-primary endpoint, it requires further justification, adjudication of events, and a clear methodological strategy.
rules.verbatim_quote: The inclusion of recurrent events as co-primary endpoint may be considered, but this setting needs further justification, adjudication of the events and a clear methodological strategy.

rules.rule_text: Exercise capacity may be considered as a primary endpoint only in selected patient populations with high unmet medical need (e.g., end stage CHF, cachexia, hypertrophic cardiomyopathies). The effect size must be clinically meaningful and consistent with improvement in patient reported outcomes.
rules.verbatim_quote: In selected patient populations with high unmet medical need (e.g. patients with end stage CHF, CHF with cachexia or hypertrophic cardiomyopathies and other specific etiologies), the effect of the treatment on exercise capacity may be considered as a primary endpoint. The effect size should be clinically meaningful and consistent with an improvement in patient reported outcomes and the cardiovascular safety profile of the product should be adequately characterised.
rules.exceptions: Further confirmation with morbidity and mortality data after registration may be required.

rules.rule_text: Patient reported outcomes (PROs) improvement in symptoms must be clinically relevant in magnitude, consistently achievable, and sustained over an extended duration of treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: Improvement in symptoms must be clinically relevant in magnitude, consistently achievable and sustained over an extended duration of treatment.

rules.rule_text: PROs are usually secondary endpoints in CHF studies. They may be acceptable as a primary endpoint only in selected populations with high unmet need where patients cannot undergo exercise testing, provided the CV safety profile is adequately characterized.
rules.verbatim_quote: PROs are usually measured as secondary endpoints in CHF studies and should be considered as supportive. In selected patient populations with high unmet medical need... measures of symptom burden may even be acceptable as a primary endpoint, provided that their outcome is consistent with other endpoints and the CV safety profile of the drug is adequately characterized.

rules.rule_text: Haemodynamic parameters cannot be used as primary endpoints in pivotal trials. The correlation between improvement in haemodynamic parameters and prognosis and/or symptoms has not been adequately established.
rules.verbatim_quote: the correlation between improvement of haemodynamic parameters and prognosis and/or symptoms has not been adequately established. Measurements of changes in haemodynamic parameters may be useful to elucidate the mode of action and the required dose of a therapeutic agent in early phase studies but cannot be used as primary endpoint in a pivotal trial.
rules.exceptions: Useful in early phase studies for mode of action and dose finding.

rules.rule_text: Biomarkers cannot be included as primary endpoints in Phase III clinical trials in CHF. None has been shown to be a reliable surrogate for clinical outcomes in the context of therapeutic interventions.
rules.verbatim_quote: none has been shown to be a reliable surrogate for clinical outcomes in the context of therapeutic interventions. To this end, biomarkers cannot be included as primary endpoints in phase III clinical trials in CHF.
rules.exceptions: Can be considered for dose selection in early drug development phases.

rules.rule_text: ICD device events used as endpoints require central adjudication and careful distinction between appropriate therapies and improper/inappropriate shocks.
rules.verbatim_quote: It will be necessary to carefully distinguish improper or inappropriate shocks from successful appropriate therapies. The inclusion of such events as endpoints needs central adjudication.

rules.rule_text: In composite endpoints with ranked hierarchy, mortality and WHF should be the first two hierarchical endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a ranked endpoint is preferred, mortality and WHF should be the first two hierarchical endpoints in the ranking procedure.

rules.rule_text: The manner in which treatment effect will be measured and quantified must be clearly specified, including handling of events occurring post-randomisation such as non-CV death.
rules.verbatim_quote: The manner in which the treatment effect will be measured and quantified should be clearly specified, in particular concerning events occurring post-randomisation such as non-CV death if this is not part of the endpoint definition.

rules.rule_text: Every effort must be made to maintain stable background therapy throughout the study. The influences of background treatment modifications on efficacy endpoints must be critically evaluated.
rules.verbatim_quote: If possible, every effort should be made during the conduct of a study in patients with CHF to maintain stable background therapy throughout the study. The influences of background treatment modifications on efficacy endpoints should be critically evaluated.

rules.rule_text: All-cause mortality may in many cases be the preferred choice over cardiovascular mortality for primary endpoint. When CV death is primary endpoint, all-cause mortality must be planned and presented as a secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: all-cause mortality may in many cases be the preferred choice. ... In this case, all-cause mortality should be planned and presented as a secondary endpoint (or as a component of a composite endpoint).
rules.exceptions: CV death as primary is acceptable when non-CV death incidence is expected to be negligible.

rules.rule_text: It is mandatory to report and centrally adjudicate all mortality data in all studies in patients with CHF. The specific cause of death must be defined.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is mandatory to report and centrally adjudicate all mortality data in all studies in patients with CHF. Efforts should be made to define the specific cause of death occurring in the studies (e.g. sudden cardiac death, pump failure, acute coronary events).

rules.rule_text: WHF events require objective evidence of cardiac decompensation as cause of worsening, with criteria rigorously defined in the protocol including signs/symptoms and biomarker changes.
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to qualify as an episode of WHF treated either with hospitalisation (HFH) or on an outpatient basis, objective evidence of cardiac de-compensation as cause of worsening should be provided. Criteria for cardiac decompensation must be rigorously defined in the protocol by signs and symptoms of deteriorating clinical conditions along with signs of cardiac overload and changes in biomarkers (see 4.1.6) as appropriate.

rules.rule_text: WHF events must be well documented and adjudicated independently and centrally, whether or not they are part of a primary composite endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: These events will need to be well documented and adjudicated independently and centrally, whether it is a component of a primary composite endpoint or not.

rules.rule_text: Reasons for changes in background therapy must always be carefully recorded with criteria pre-specified in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: the reasons for a change in the background therapy should always be carefully recorded and the criteria for these events must be pre-specified in the protocol.

rules.rule_text: Patients must be followed for events of interest regardless of adherence to randomized treatment, with all events included in the primary analysis unless otherwise justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients should be followed for events of interest regardless of adherence to randomized treatment, with all events included in the primary analysis unless otherwise justified.

rules.rule_text: Hospitalisation variability across regions must be addressed by implementing similar criteria for hospitalisation and stratification by regions.
rules.verbatim_quote: the threshold for hospitalisation is highly variable across (and within) regions of the world which may affect the interpretability and applicability of study results to the European population. This should be taken into account when planning the studies, e.g. by implementing similar criteria for hospitalisation and stratification by regions.

rules.rule_text: Exercise testing must be performed using protocols specifically designed for functional assessment of CHF patients. Reasons for termination of sub-maximal exercise tests must be specified a priori.
rules.verbatim_quote: Exercise testing should be performed using appropriate protocols specifically designed for the functional assessment of patients with CHF (5). The reason(s) for termination of sub-maximal exercise tests should be specified a priori.

rules.rule_text: Symptom assessment scales must be validated in the populations and languages being tested. NYHA class must always be documented as a standard to allow comparisons across trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Other scales or scores can be used for the assessment of symptoms provided that they are validated in the populations (and in the languages) in which they are being tested. Whatever scale is used, it must be capable of providing robust evidence of symptomatic improvement. However, NYHA class as an established standard should always be documented to allow comparisons across trials.

rules.rule_text: QoL questionnaires must be fully validated for the disease (e.g., Minnesota Living with Heart Failure, Kansas City Cardiomyopathy Questionnaire), translated and validated in all languages spoken by patients in the clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Questionnaires to be used must be fully validated for the disease (e.g. Minnesota Living with Heart Failure Questionnaire or Kansas City Cardiomyopathy Questionnaire). In order to be considered, questionnaires must be translated and validated in all the languages spoken in the countries of patients included in the clinical studies.

rules.rule_text: Haemodynamic measurement techniques must be validated beforehand and justified. Investigators must specify norms and inter-/intra-operator variability. Variability should be reduced by core laboratory analyses.
rules.verbatim_quote: New techniques used to study the haemodynamic effect of an agent in CHF must be validated beforehand and justified. ... Given the inter-operator variability, the investigators from each centre should specify the norms for their laboratory and the inter- as well as intra-operator variability. Variability can be reduced by core laboratory analyses.

rules.rule_text: Patients must be differentiated by degree of LVEF preservation: HFrEF (LVEF <40%), HFmrEF (LVEF 40-49%), and HFpEF (LVEF >=50%). EF cut-offs must be defined before inclusion in the study.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with CHF must be differentiated according to the degree of preservation of left ventricular function (left ventricular ejection fraction [LVEF]) between those with reduced ejection fraction ([HFrEF]; LVEF <40%), those with mid-range ejection fraction ([HFmrEF]; LVEF 40-49%) and those with normal/preserved LVEF ([HFpEF], typically considered as LVEF ≥50%). ... The cut-offs for the ejection fraction (EF) should be defined before inclusion in the study and discussed.

rules.rule_text: Patients entering Phase IIb and III trials must be NYHA class II-IV and treated at study entry as per clinical practice guidelines. A sizeable number must be representative for the European population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients entering phase IIb and III clinical trials with agents for the treatment of CHF (NYHA class II-IV) should be treated at study entry as per clinical practice guidelines. Given the worldwide variability in therapeutic practices a sizeable number of patients included in clinical trials should be representative for the European population with regards to their background treatment and standard of care.

rules.rule_text: A period of stability of CHF medications is required before inclusion, unless not feasible due to new diagnosis or recent acute decompensation.
rules.verbatim_quote: For studies to be conducted in patients with CHF, a period of stability of CHF medications is required before inclusion, unless not feasible due to new diagnosed disease or a recent acute decompensation.
rules.exceptions: New diagnosis or recent acute decompensation.

rules.rule_text: Confirmatory studies must be controlled, double blind, randomised. Stratified allocation is mandatory for gender.
rules.verbatim_quote: Controlled, double blind, randomised studies are required. ... Stratified allocation is mandatory for gender and may sometimes be desirable for the other factor listed above.

rules.rule_text: At least one controlled study of a minimum duration of 6 months is mandatory to demonstrate efficacy in relation to functional benefit as primary endpoint. Sufficient data to characterise the cardiovascular safety profile will be needed before approval.
rules.verbatim_quote: At least one controlled study of a minimum duration of 6 months is mandatory to demonstrate efficacy in relation to functional benefit when this is the primary endpoint. In this case sufficient data to characterise the cardiovascular safety profile will be needed before approval.

rules.rule_text: If approval is based on functional and/or symptomatic data without a cardiovascular outcome study, cardiovascular and all-cause mortality data must still be available in the database with an acceptable non-inferiority margin to ensure the CV safety profile is adequately characterized.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the basis for an approval is functional and/or symptomatic data, without a cardiovascular outcome study, cardiovascular and all cause mortality data are expected to be available in the database in order to ensure that the cardiovascular safety profile is adequately characterized. Such data could arise either from several trials or alternatively within the pivotal study by the use of (cardiovascular/all-cause) mortality as a secondary endpoint with a well defined and an acceptable non-inferiority margin.
